mentions.note: CEO Lisa Su is excellent; competitive vs. Nvidia as second-best in semiconductor industry. Cramer won't add due to large existing Nvidia position but would buy if not already heavily weighted.
Excellent bank at current 47 level; could test 40 but fundamentally sound. Trading at 10x earnings; think long-term and accumulate.
Legacy packaged food company owning B&M Baked Beans and Underwood Deviled Ham. Sell; long history of disappointing results and execution; not worth investing in.
Recommended alternative to standalone insurance plays; diversified insurance exposure through GEICO and other holdings.
Down from $26 to $16; 8.25% yield reflects deep valuation cut, not growth. Guidance flat (+1% to -1% sales). Consolidation candidate.

Optical components company joining S&P 500 this month alongside Lumentum; received $2B Nvidia investment; riding same AI data center tailwind.
Hit 17-year low on worst quarter in ages: revenues -5%, organic sales -3%, snack business collapsing, dividend sustainability questioned. Consolidation candidate.
Buy and hold for yield in current inflationary environment; great stock despite parabolic move. Buy on any weakness; benefits from elevated oil prices.
Hold current position; war fears creating artificial selling pressure, but this is quality industrial stock worth owning long-term. Don't panic sell on geopolitical uncertainty.
Weak cereal demand with heavy discounting; cut earnings guidance. Stock impacted by weak consumer sentiment and volatility. Consolidation candidate under Steve Cahillane plan.

CEO Steve Cahillane only recent food executive to create shareholder value; track record splitting Kellogg and achieving major exits. Consolidation hub candidate to acquire Campbell, General Mills, Conagra.

Optical connectivity supplier completely sold out through end of 2027; 25-30% behind demand. Up 900% in 12 months; $2B Nvidia investment validates thesis. 5+ year AI data center buildout visibility with no end in sight.
Up 45% YTD; trim half of position to lock in gains and play remainder with house money. Stock has not been great historically—don't chase pure momentum on tanker cycle recovery.
Driving AI data center transformation; optical suppliers Lumentum and Coherent dependent on Nvidia's chip roadmap and network architectures for long-term growth.
Caller Bill holds 1,500 shares from 2008 at $40, now at $160 with 3.5% yield. CEO Ramon Laguarta executing well; hold despite war fears—don't sell quality on short-term uncertainty.
Caller Miguel asked about Progressive Insurance; Cramer prefers Berkshire Hathaway or Chubb for insurance exposure over standalone insurers.
Minerals and metals play with improving fundamentals; company could break even or slightly exceed. Hold existing position; emerging opportunity in commodity space.
